items.label: Demo Pulse
items.focus: Compare autoplay cadence and whether the board feels mechanically alive enough once demo motion begins.

window.selection: highest-scoring non-overlapping reference subwindow after explicit Aurora audio-theme capture; original 4.148s window plus 0.450s-0.690s candidate

items.label: Stage Start
items.focus: Compare whether stage start feels like a clear arcade start/announcement instead of a generic cue.

window.selection: highest-scoring non-overlapping reference subwindow after explicit Aurora audio-theme capture

items.label: Formation Pulse
items.focus: Compare ongoing formation cadence and whether gameplay feels like it has enough marching pressure.

window.selection: highest-scoring non-overlapping reference subwindow after explicit Aurora audio-theme capture

items.label: Player Shot
items.focus: Compare whether player fire reads as a quick arcade shot instead of a generic impact.

window.selection: distinct initial high-transient subwindow reserved for player fire; lower score than the shared best window but prevents playerShot/enemyShot/bossHit semantic collapse

segmentation.resolution: relative to the clipped 0.05-0.29s flagship/fighter-shot window; player fire is a single crisp transient rather than a multi-phase phrase

items.label: Enemy Shot
items.focus: Compare whether enemy fire is distinct enough from player fire and boss damage.

window.selection: second measured high-transient subwindow reserved for enemy projectile feedback so enemyShot no longer scores against the same active phrase as playerShot

segmentation.resolution: relative to the clipped 0.90-1.14s flagship/fighter-shot window; enemy fire is a single projectile transient

items.label: Enemy Hit
items.focus: Compare whether an enemy impact gives immediate hit confirmation.

window.selection: later Zako phrase reserved for immediate hit confirmation, separated from the normal-enemy destruction subwindow

segmentation.resolution: relative to the clipped 0.75-0.99s Zako window; enemy hit is scored as immediate damage confirmation

items.label: Boss Hit
items.focus: Compare whether boss damage reads as a distinct multi-hit event.

window.selection: highest-scoring measured subwindow for boss damage confirmation, reserved for multi-hit boss feedback rather than generic shot identity

segmentation.resolution: relative to the clipped 1.149-1.439s boss-damage window; boss hit is a distinct damage confirmation transient

items.label: Enemy Boom
items.focus: Compare whether normal enemy destruction is more emphatic than a simple hit.

window.selection: highest-scoring measured Zako subwindow reserved for normal-enemy destruction, separated from enemyHit

segmentation.source: curated from aurora-audio-theme-comparison/2026-05-09-main-6c0e2b61 measured Zako destruction transient
segmentation.resolution: relative to the clipped 0.50-0.74s Zako window; normal enemy destruction is a compact finality transient

items.label: Boss Boom
items.focus: Compare whether boss destruction feels larger and more final than normal enemy destruction.

window.selection: full active boss-death phrase so destruction can be scored as a larger multi-burst event rather than a single generic explosion

segmentation.resolution: relative to the clipped 0.798-1.567s boss-death window; labels early, middle, and final destruction bursts

items.label: Capture Beam
items.focus: Compare whether the beam deploy moment reads clearly as tractor-beam danger.

window.selection: highest-scoring non-overlapping reference subwindow after explicit Aurora audio-theme capture

segmentation.source: curated from measured tractor-beam reference window after repeatability-gate pass
segmentation.resolution: relative to the clipped 3.199-3.679s tractor-beam window; labels separate beam strike from sustained beam body

items.label: Capture Retreat
items.focus: Compare whether the post-capture retreat phase remains audible as a distinct state.

window.selection: highest-scoring non-overlapping reference subwindow after explicit Aurora audio-theme capture

items.label: Fighter Captured
items.focus: Compare the exact moment the fighter is fully captured, distinct from beam deploy and retreat.

window.selection: highest-scoring non-overlapping reference subwindow after explicit Aurora audio-theme capture

items.label: Captured Fighter Destroyed
items.focus: Compare the regret/penalty moment when the carried fighter is destroyed.

window.selection: highest-scoring non-overlapping reference subwindow after explicit Aurora audio-theme capture

segmentation.resolution: relative to the clipped 1.949-2.189s captured-fighter-destroyed window; penalty feedback is a compact regret/destruction transient

items.label: Rescue Join
items.focus: Compare whether the rescue/join moment feels celebratory and distinct from ordinary scoring.

window.selection: highest-scoring stable keeper after focused rescueJoin candidate loop and full-theme promotion precheck

items.label: Ship Loss
items.focus: Compare whether ship loss feels like a real Galaga death moment rather than a generic impact.

analysisPolicy.reason: Ship loss is a composite onset/body/tail event; conformance should score the scheduled cue window rather than the single loudest active island.

segmentation.source: curated from active-window reads in aurora-audio-theme-comparison and repeated playerHit candidate pass
segmentation.resolution: relative to full galaga3-death clip; labels preserve long death onset, sustained body, and final tail

items.label: Challenge Transition
items.focus: Compare how distinct and ceremonial the challenge-stage announcement feels.

window.selection: highest-scoring non-overlapping reference subwindow after explicit Aurora audio-theme capture

items.label: Challenge Results
items.focus: Compare whether challenge wrap-up feels like a proper result phrase instead of a generic transition.

window.selection: highest-scoring non-overlapping reference subwindow after explicit Aurora audio-theme capture

segmentation.source: curated from challenging-stage-results reference window after challengeResults event-gap pass
segmentation.resolution: relative to the clipped 0.75-1.17s challenge-results phrase; labels distinguish attack, result body, and falloff

items.label: Challenge Perfect

items.label: High Score 1st

items.focus: Compare whether non-first leaderboard entry feels positive but smaller than first place.

items.label: Game Over
items.focus: Compare whether the ending cue feels final and emotionally complete enough.

window.selection: highest-scoring non-overlapping reference subwindow after explicit Aurora audio-theme capture
